questions.question: Where is the headquarters of UNESCO located?
questions.explanation: Recently, Khaled el-Anani, former Egyptian Minister of Tourism and Antiquities, was nominated as the next Director of UNESCO by its executive board. UNESCO stands for United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization and is a specialized agency of the United Nations. Established in 1945 after World War II, it succeeded the International Committee on Intellectual Cooperation. The headquarters of UNESCO is in Paris, France. It has 194 member countries and 12 associate members.

questions.question: Which country has allocated 10 million US dollars to boost community resilience to natural disasters across Asia and the Pacific?
questions.explanation: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced a 10 million US dollars contribution to strengthen community resilience against natural disasters across Asia and the Pacific. The initiative was launched through the UAE Aid Agency during the 2025 International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) World Conservation Congress in Abu Dhabi. It will support Emirates Nature–World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF) under the Community Resilience to Natural Disasters Programme, developed with International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC). The programme focuses on restoring mangroves and coral reefs, promoting eco-livelihoods, and strengthening disaster preparedness in Philippines, Indonesia, Fiji, and Solomon Islands.

questions.question: Which country has become the first in Latin America to legalise euthanasia through legislation?
questions.explanation: Uruguay has become the first country in Latin America to legalise euthanasia through the Dignified Death Bill. The law allows mentally sound adults with terminal, irreversible diseases to request assisted suicide from a healthcare professional. Uruguay, known for socially liberal laws like marijuana legalisation, same-sex marriage, and abortion, now adds euthanasia.

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the International Maritime Organization (IMO) located?
questions.explanation: United States President Donald Trump recently criticized the International Maritime Organization (IMO) for considering a carbon emissions price on global shipping, calling it a “Global Green New Scam Tax.” The IMO is a United Nations (UN) specialized agency responsible for shipping safety, security, and prevention of marine pollution. It has 176 Member States and 3 Associate Members, headquartered in London. IMO sets global standards for shipping safety, security, and environmental performance, contributing to UN Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 14. Its Assembly and Council govern the organization, while five major committees, including the Marine Environment Protection Committee (MEPC), develop policies and regulations.

questions.question: Which country has officially become the 26th country to eliminate trachoma as a public health concern?
questions.explanation: Fiji has officially become the 26th country to eliminate trachoma as a public health concern. Trachoma is a neglected tropical disease (NTD) and the world’s leading infectious cause of blindness. It is caused by Chlamydia trachomatis bacteria. The disease spreads through personal contact, contaminated surfaces, and flies exposed to eye or nasal discharge. Trachoma is endemic in communities with limited clean water and sanitation access. India has also successfully eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. Fiji’s achievement reflects progress in global NTD control and eye health initiatives.

questions.question: Which international organization recognized India’s Duty-Free Tariff Preference (DFTP) scheme for boosting exports from poorest nations?

options.label: C
options.text: United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD)

questions.explanation: The World Trade Organization (WTO) recently credited India’s Duty-Free Tariff Preference (DFTP) scheme for boosting exports from Least Developed Countries (LDCs). The DFTP scheme started in 2008 and provides preferential access to the Indian market for LDCs. The objective is to promote economic growth, diversify exports, and strengthen trade relations of LDCs. Duty-Free Access allows products from LDCs to enter India without import duties. The scheme helps LDCs expand trade and integrate into the global economy.

questions.question: The Kunar River, that was recently seen in news, is a tributary of which river?
questions.explanation: Afghanistan has announced plans to construct a dam on the Kunar River, blocking its flow into Pakistan. The Kunar River is a major tributary of the Kabul River, which later merges with the Indus River in Pakistan. The river originates from the glaciated Hindu Kush Mountains in Chitral, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan. It flows into Afghanistan and joins the Kabul River there. The two main tributaries of the Kunar River are Bashgal (Landiasind) River and Pech River. The proposed dam may affect water flow and relations between Afghanistan and Pakistan.

questions.question: Which country has unveiled the world’s fastest bullet train, the CR450?
questions.explanation: China has started pre-service trials for its new high-speed train CR450, announced on October 21, 2025. The CR450 is hailed as the world’s fastest bullet train. During trials on the Shanghai–Chongqing–Chengdu high-speed railway line, it reached a top speed of 281 miles per hour (450 km/h). The CR450 marks a major step in China’s next-generation high-speed rail technology. It aims to improve travel efficiency and connectivity across major Chinese cities. Once operational, it will surpass the speed record of China’s current CR400 series trains.

questions.question: Which country has become the first in South Asia to join the United Nations (UN) Water Convention?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh became the first country in South Asia to join the United Nations (UN) Water Convention. The UN Water Convention is officially called the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses and International Lakes. It was adopted in Helsinki in 1992 and came into force in 1996. Initially designed for the pan-European region, it became open to all UN Member States after March 2016. It is a legally binding treaty promoting sustainable management of shared water resources. It helps prevent conflicts, promote peace, and support the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

questions.question: Which country has been elected to the International Coordinating Council of UNESCO’s Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme for 2025–2029?
questions.explanation: Oman has been elected to the International Coordinating Council of UNESCO’s Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme for the 2025–2029 term. The election took place during the 43rd UNESCO General Conference in Samarkand, Uzbekistan. The Environment Authority of Oman represented the Sultanate at the session. This achievement aligns with Oman Vision 2040 and the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The MAB Programme promotes scientific collaboration to conserve biodiversity, manage protected areas, and support climate research. It also empowers youth and researchers through grants and promotes sustainable environmental practices globally.
